name: recovery
description: Recover session state from memory-keeper after context loss.
capabilities: Retrieves recent context items from memory-keeper database
Identifies files modified in previous sessions
Locates next action items and blockers
Presents recovered state in human-readable format
author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc
securitySummary: All 31 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. SKILL.md contains only documentation for memory-keeper MCP tools using markdown code fences. The 'backticks' flagged are markdown delimiters, not shell execution. Function names like 'context_get' contain 'get' which triggered false 'cryptographic' flags. No code execution, network calls, filesystem access, or external commands exist. The skill-report.json already contains an accurate security audit confirming 'safe' status.
valueStatement: Context loss disrupts workflow and wastes time recreating progress. This skill retrieves saved checkpoints and progress items from memory-keeper to restore session state instantly.
limitations: Requires memory-keeper MCP server to be configured
Cannot recover context from sessions that did not checkpoint
Cannot restore if memory was explicitly cleared
Only works with context items stored by memory-keeper

useCases.title: Resume interrupted work
useCases.description: Continue coding sessions after context exhaustion without losing progress or manually tracking state.

useCases.title: Restore from checkpoints
useCases.description: Get quick summary of where work left off when reconnecting to an agent session.

useCases.title: Track team progress
useCases.description: Understand current task status and next steps when reviewing developer session history.

promptTemplates.title: Basic recovery
promptTemplates.prompt: Recover my session context. What was I working on?

promptTemplates.title: After context loss
promptTemplates.prompt: Restore from my last checkpoint. Show me what files I modified and what I need to do next.

promptTemplates.title: Progress check
promptTemplates.prompt: Give me a summary of my current task progress and next action.

promptTemplates.title: Recovery verification
promptTemplates.prompt: Verify my recovered context and confirm the next step before I proceed.

bestPractices: Checkpoint frequently during complex tasks using memory-keeper
Review recovered context before continuing work
Use consistent naming conventions for checkpoints
antiPatterns: Working without checkpointing before long operations
Assuming all session data is automatically saved

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with all Claude products?
faq.answer: Yes, works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code when memory-keeper MCP is configured.

faq.question: What data does this skill access?
faq.answer: Only context items stored in memory-keeper. No files, environment variables, or network calls.

faq.question: Can I recover context from yesterday?
faq.answer: Yes, if memory-keeper MCP was running and checkpointing during your session.

faq.question: What happens if no context is found?
faq.answer: The skill presents a clear message explaining why no context exists and offers next steps.

faq.question: How do I integrate this with my workflow?
faq.answer: Use memory-keeper checkpoint functions regularly. Run recovery at the start of new sessions.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This is a read-only skill that only retrieves your own stored context data.
